content: i've been generating creative work since i was around 12 years old; i started releasing music in my twenties, and due to the nature of our digital age, i've looked back at a body of work that never never launched me into super stardom as a failure, instinctually i'd remove it or purge it from the artistic record and thought, that must've not been good work; but i also felt, hey, that was the absolute best i was able to do at the time, and it all becomes another brick in my wall; now, returning to staten island in fall 2024 after a suicide attempt, my first return since 2021 (after moving to Texas with Madison from florida for her phd, our stuff destroyed in a moving accident, and broken up with) after being away since 2014 as i left for my MFA @ FAU (2015-18), a self exile method of escape to run from my childhood home, but a cowardly move as i didn't want to continue school, i wanted to buckle down and be the artist i've always been (this pattern of fear forcing a move to avoid the hard part is never ending); the first piece of creative advice i ever received and first piece of advice i avoided was from on writing by stephen king, write what you know, and i don't like what i
context: From document: i've been generating creative work since i was around 12 years old… — chunk 1 (chunk 1/13)
